name: Pesto Risotto
recipeIngredient: 2 Tablespoons Pine nuts, or chopped walnuts
4 Cups Chicken Broth, homemade, or canned (low-sodium)
recipeInstructions:
Prepare Pesto: In a food processor fitted with a chopping blade, process basil leaves, cheese, parsley sprigs, pine nuts or chopped walnuts, and garlic until a smooth paste forms. Gradually add olive oil and process until well blended. Set aside.
In a 2-quart saucepan, heat broth and wine just to boiling. Remove from heat. In a large skillet, heat oil over medium heat. Add rice and garlic; sauté, stirring constantly, for 3 minutes or until the rice just starts to brown. Add half a cup of the hot broth mixture; cook, stirring constantly, until all the liquid has been absorbed. Continue to cook, stirring constantly and adding broth mixture, one half cup at a time, until all liquid has been absorbed and the rice is creamy but slightly al dente—about 20 to 25 minutes. If desired, add salt to taste. Pour risotto into a serving dish. Spoon pesto onto the risotto in a spiral pattern; top with basil sprig and serve immediately. (It will become sticky if allowed to stand before serving.)
